title: Lumen — a from-scratch path tracer
description: A physically based path tracer built from scratch in TypeScript with THREE selectable light-transport integrators: a unidirectional Monte-Carlo path tracer, a full bidirectional path tracer (BDPT) — Veach–Guibas camera×light vertex connections weighted by balance-heuristic multiple importance sampling, which renders indirect-lit scenes (an emitter hidden in a cove) at a fraction of the noise — AND primary-sample-space Metropolis light transport (PSSMLT), a Markov chain run over the path tracer's own random-number stream (Kelemen mutation + Metropolis-Hastings acceptance, bootstrap normalisation, expected-value splatting, parallel chains across the worker pool) that locks onto the hardest-to-find light and refines the whole frame at once — the path tracer is reused verbatim as the contribution function because the Metropolis sampler simply IS the RNG. Because all three estimators solve the same rendering equation, the in-app verification suite proves they converge to the SAME image (checking PSSMLT against the path tracer on both global brightness and spatial distribution) and that the MIS weights form an exact partition of unity. Plus participating media (fog/smoke/cloud) with Henyey-Greenstein scattering and volumetric god rays, thin-film interference for soap-bubble/oil-slick iridescence, smooth triangle meshes (icosphere, lathe & OBJ import) with barycentric-interpolated normals, a SAH bounding volume hierarchy, GGX microfacet BSDFs with VNDF sampling, smooth and frosted dielectrics, spectral dispersion (prism rainbows), Beer-Lambert volumetric absorption, procedural textures, an analytic Preetham daylight sky whose sun is a sampled light, next-event estimation with multiple importance sampling, scrambled-Halton low-discrepancy sampling, adaptive variance-guided sampling with a live noise heatmap, a multi-threaded Web Worker pool, ACES tone mapping, and an edge-avoiding À-Trous denoiser — global illumination on the CPU, in your browser.
